metadata.name: racetime-bot
metadata.summary: Foundation system for creating chat bots for racetime.gg

functions.name: racetime_bot.can_moderate
documentation:
Determine if the user who sent the message is a moderator.

Returns False if moderator status is indeterminate, e.g. message was sent
by a bot instead of a user.

functions.name: racetime_bot.can_monitor
documentation:
Determine if the user who sent the message is a race monitor.

Returns False if monitor status is indeterminate, e.g. message was sent
by a bot instead of a user.

functions.name: racetime_bot.moderator_cmd
documentation:
Restrict a command so it can only be used by category moderators.

Use as a decorator in your race handler, for example:

@moderator_cmd
async def ex_lock(self, args, message):
    await self.send_message('...')

functions.name: racetime_bot.monitor_cmd
documentation:
Restrict a command so it can only be used by race monitors.

Use as a decorator in your race handler, for example:

@moderator_cmd
async def ex_lock(self, args, message):
    await self.send_message('...')

modules.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions
documentation:
These classes help you build up actions to attach to chat messages.

An action button will give a user a quick way to send a particular message that
your bot can then act on. Think of it like a shortcut to using the text
commands.

Actions can optionally include a Survey, which allows you to customise what
message is sent by first giving the user a small form to fill in. The answers
will then be interpolated into the action message.

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.Action
documentation:
An action button.

`label` (required) - this is what appears on the button
`message` (required) - what message the user will send when they click this button.
                       Use interpolation to include survey answers, e.g. "!seed ${preset}"
`submit` (optional) - when using a survey, this text appears on the submit button
`survey` (optional) - should be a Survey instance
`help_text` (optional) - title text that appears when user hovers on the button

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.ActionLink
documentation:
An action link.

`label` (required) - this is what appears on the button
`url` (required) - where the button links to
`help_text` (optional) - title text that appears when user hovers on the button

Note that link actions cannot use surveys.

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.Survey
documentation: `questions` should be Question instances

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.Question
documentation:
Abstract Question class. Use one of the Input classes for actual surveys.

All questions have the following parameters:

`name` (required) - used to identify the value when sending the message
`label` (required) - friendly name of the field the user sees
`default` (optional) - Default value for the field
`help_text` (optional) - Extra text that appears below the field

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.TextInput
documentation:
A free-text field.

In addition to the parameters listed above, a TextInput also has:

`placeholder` (optional) - Placeholder text for the field

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.BoolInput
documentation:
A checkbox button.

BoolInput has no additional parameters.

Note that `default`.

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.RadioInput
documentation:
A set of radio buttons.

In addition to the parameters listed above, a RadioInput also has:

`options` (required) - A dict of {value: label} pairs

Note that if `default` is used, its value should match a value from `options`, not a label.

classes.name: racetime_bot.msg_actions.SelectInput
documentation:
A drop-down select box.

In addition to the parameters listed above, a SelectInput also has:

`options` (required) - A dict of {value: label} pairs

Note that if `default` is used, its value should match a value from `options`, not a label.

classes.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.__init__
documentation: Bot constructor.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.get_handler_class
documentation:
Returns the handler class for races. Each race the bot finds will have
its own handler object of this class.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.get_handler_kwargs
documentation:
Returns a dict of keyword arguments to be passed into the handler class
when instantiated.

Override this method if you need to pass additional kwargs to your race
handler.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.should_handle
documentation:
Determine if a race we've found should be handled by this bot or not.

Returns True if the race should have a handler created for it, False
otherwise.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.authorize
documentation: Get an OAuth2 token from the authentication server.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.create_handler
documentation:
Create a new WebSocket connection and set up a handler object to manage
it.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.reauthorize
documentation:
Reauthorize with the token endpoint, to generate a new access token
before the current one expires.

This method runs in a constant loop, creating a new access token when
needed.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.refresh_races
documentation:
Retrieve current race information from the category detail API
endpoint, retrieving the current race list. Creates a handler and task
for any race that should be handled but currently isn't.

This method runs in a constant loop, checking for new races every few
seconds.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.handle_exception
documentation: Handle exceptions that occur during the event loop.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.run
documentation: Run the bot. Creates an event loop then iterates over it forever.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.http_uri
documentation: Generate a HTTP/HTTPS URI from the given URL path fragment.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.ws_uri
documentation: Generate a WS/WSS URI from the given URL path fragment.

methods.name: racetime_bot.bot.Bot.uri
documentation: Generate a URI from the given protocol and URL path fragment.

The racetime.gg bot class.

This bot uses event loops to connect to multiple race rooms at once. Each
room is assigned a handler object, which you can determine with the
`get_handler_class`/`get_handler_kwargs` methods.

When implementing your own bot, you will need to specify what handler you
wish to use, as the default `RaceHandler` will not actually do anything
other than connect to the room and log the messages.

classes.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.__init__
documentation:
Base handler constructor.

Sets up the following attributes:
* conn - WebSocket connection, used internally.
* data - Race data dict, as retrieved from race detail API endpoint.
* logger - The logger instance bot was instantiated with.
* state - A dict of stateful data for this race
* ws - The open WebSocket, used internally.

About data vs state - data is the race information retrieved from the
server and can be read by your handler, but should not be written to.
The state on the other hand can be used by your handler to preserve
information about the race. It is preserved even if the handler is
recreated (e.g. due to disconnect). Use it for any information you
want.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.should_stop
documentation:
Determine if the handler should be terminated. This is checked after
every receieved message.

By default, checks if the race state matches one of the values in
`stop_at`.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.begin
documentation:
Bot actions to perform when first connecting to a race room.

Override this method to add an intro for when your bot first appears.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.consume
documentation:
Standard message consumer. This is called for every message we receive
from the site.

This implementation will attempt to find an appropriate method to call
to handle the incoming data, based on its type. For example if we have
a "race.data" type message, it will call `self.race_data(data)`.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.end
documentation:
Bot actions to perform just before disconnecting from a race room.

Override this method to add an outro for when your bot leaves.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.error
documentation:
Consume an incoming "error" type message.

By default, just raises the message as an exception.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.chat_message
documentation:
Consume an incoming "chat.message" type message.

This method assumes a standard bot operation. It checks the first word
in the message, and if it looks like an exclaimation command like
"!seed", then it will call the relevant method, i.e.
`self.ex_seed(args, message)` (where `args` is the remainder of the
message split up by words, and message is the original message blob).

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.race_data
documentation:
Consume an incoming "race.data" message.

By default just updates the `data` attribute on the object. If you
want to react to race changes, you can override this method to add
further functionality.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.send_message
documentation:
Send a chat message to the race room.

`message` should be the message string you want to send.
`actions` should be a list of Action objects (or raw dict data, if you're bold).
`pinned` will pin the message at the top of the chat window.
`direct_to` will send message as DM to the specified user ID.

Note: for more info on setting up race actions, see `msg_actions.py`

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.set_bot_raceinfo
documentation: Set the `info_bot` field on the race room's data.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.set_raceinfo
documentation:
Set the `info_user` field on the race room's data.

This method is deprecated, please switch to using `set_bot_raceinfo`.

`info` should be the information you wish to set. By default, this
method will prefix your information with the existing info, if needed.
You can change this to suffix with `prefix=False`, or disable this
behaviour entirely with `overwrite=True`.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.set_open
documentation: Set the room in an open state.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.set_invitational
documentation: Set the room in an invite-only state.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.force_start
documentation: Forces a start of the race.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.cancel_race
documentation: Forcibly cancels a race.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.invite_user
documentation:
Invites a user to the race.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.accept_request
documentation:
Accepts a request to join the race room.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.force_unready
documentation:
Forcibly unreadies an entrant.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.remove_entrant
documentation:
Forcibly removes an entrant from the race.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.add_monitor
documentation:
Adds a user as a race monitor.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.remove_monitor
documentation:
Removes a user as a race monitor.

`user` should be the hashid of the user.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.pin_message
documentation:
Pin a chat message.

`message` should be the hashid of the message.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.unpin_message
documentation:
Unpin a chat message.

`message` should be the hashid of the message.

methods.name: racetime_bot.handler.RaceHandler.handle
documentation:
Low-level handler for the race room. This will loop over the websocket,
processing any messages that come in.

Standard race handler.

You should use this class as a basis for creating your own handler that
can consume incoming messages, react to race data changes, and send stuff
back to the race room.
